title: Configuring the Debugger Error and Exception behavior
breadcrumbs: Configuring the Debugger Error and Exception behavior
text: Configuring the Debugger Error and Exception behavior The following procedure allows developers to enable or disable Uniface debugger breakpoints based on the defined errors or exceptions break-on conditions on the Settings > Error configuration tab. On the Uniface Debugger window, navigate to View > Settings . The system displays the Settings dialog box. Click the Errors tab. The system displays the Errors screen. Select any of the following required conditions: Do not break : Use this option to disable debugger breaks on any error or exception, irrespective of the specific Proc error codes defined under the Specific errors radio option. All errors regarded as exceptions : Use this option to enable debugger breaks on all errors that occur in exception-enabled code, irrespective of the specific Proc error codes defined under the Specific errors radio option. All errors : Use this option to enable debugger breaks on all errors, irrespective of the specific Proc error codes defined under the Specific errors radio option. Specific errors : Use this option to enable debugger breaks on the Proc error codes defined under this option. When you select this option, you must add or remove error numbers as per the requirement. Click Apply to activate the new configuration changes. You can now compile your codes and test your settings.
